Tests exception handling with a try-catch block in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that demonstrates basic exception handling in JavaScript. The [`test`](<#13-001test>) function attempts to execute a block of code that deliberately throws an error when the condition `if (true)` is met. The `catch` block captures the thrown error and returns `true`, indicating that the error was successfully caught. If no error is thrown, the function returns `false`. The `assert` function is then used to verify that the [`test`](<#13-001test>) function returns `true`, confirming that the error handling mechanism works as expected.
# Functions

### test<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/12/12.13/12.13-001.test}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/12/12.13/12.13-001.js#L15>)

Evaluates a condition and returns a boolean based on error handling.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Control Flow**:
    - Enters a try block.
    - Evaluates a condition that is always true.
    - Throws an error if the condition is true.
    - Catches the error in the catch block.
    - Returns true from the catch block if an error is caught.
    - Returns false if no error is caught.
- **Output**: A boolean value, true if an error is caught, otherwise false.
